purpose: Plan a multi-channel content or marketing campaign end-to-end, from ideation through scheduled publication.
primaryUserGoal: Move a campaign from initial brainstorm to a scheduled publishing plan with drafts and projections.
whenHermesShouldChoose: User asks to plan a launch, build a content calendar, manage a marketing campaign, or organize content ideas across channels.
supports: Tabs: Ideas (card-grid with thumbnails), Drafts (notes per draft), Schedule (timeline), Notes
idealDataShape: 5-10 content ideas, each with image.query and format tag
Projected impressions over the campaign window

populationInstructions.summary: Create a tabs section: Ideas (card-grid with image.query thumbnails, one per piece), Drafts (notes with body drafts), Schedule (timeline of publish dates), Notes. Include a line-chart of projected impressions.
steps: Parse campaign goal, window, channels (blog, social, email).
Ideas tab: 5-10 cards each with image.query thumbnail and 2-3 bullets (format, angle, channel).
Drafts tab: notes section with per-draft placeholders.
Schedule tab: timeline with one item per publish event, labeled with date/time.
Include a line-chart with projected weekly impressions across the campaign window.
Notes tab: audience insights, competitor moves, review dates.
guardrails: Never project impressions without noting they're estimates.
Don't schedule more than 1-2 publishes per day unless asked.
Keep idea bullets focused on angle, not aspiration.

patchPrefer: Use upsert_cards when adding or refining ideas
Use upsert_timeline_items when the schedule shifts
Use append_note_lines for drafts and retrospectives
Use set_active_tab when the user changes focus
replaceTriggers: User pivots to a new campaign
